notes: Avoids native-picker ordering and opening a dialog after the user has committed to start.

expected: Selecting any one specification-named part discovers its siblings and reconstructs them in numeric order.
notes: The user never has to depend on native-picker ordering.

expected: Duplicate selections and parts from differently named sets fail before any output is published.
notes: Keeps ambiguous sets out of libpna sequence handling.

expected: The selected tool explains its output boundary and preservation or removal effect before Start becomes available.
notes: The summary changes with the selected operation and does not claim unsupported preservation.

expected: Timestamps, permission facets, xattrs, and private chunks are removed or retained exactly as selected while the source remains byte-identical.
notes: The test builds metadata in the archive directly and is independent of host filesystem xattr support.

expected: Cancellation before publication removes every temporary or public part and leaves the source byte-identical.
notes: Exercises cancellation at the post-entry safe boundary.

expected: Cancellation removes the separate partial output and leaves the source byte-identical.
notes: The source and output directory are asserted directly.

expected: Each tool preserves readable encrypted solid storage with the correct password; a wrong password publishes no output.
notes: Source bytes are compared after all three operations.

expected: Every rename or delete progress update uses the verified logical item count rather than reporting each intermediate item as 100 percent.
notes: Asserts the exact completion and total sequence.

expected: Every normalization progress update uses the verified logical item count.
notes: Encrypted solid preflight uses the same count path before output creation.

expected: A failed delete submission keeps the exact destructive scope dialog open and shows the actionable error without losing selection.
notes: Prevents a closed confirmation from looking like successful deletion.

expected: A failed append submission keeps the selected source paths and an inline actionable error in the dialog.
notes: The user can retry without reopening native pickers.

expected: A failed normalization submission keeps the selected operation and output path with an inline actionable error.
notes: The user can retry without reopening the native save dialog.

expected: A completed split job offers reveal-in-folder but never tries to open part 1 as a complete archive.
notes: Concat and normalization outputs remain directly openable.

expected: A wrong password is rejected before indexing an encrypted uncompressed entry, and ciphertext is never exposed as preview text.
notes: PNA GUI plaintext integrity metadata is verified; legacy encrypted stored entries without an integrity digest fail closed.

expected: JavaScript module source files are recognized as safe text preview candidates.
notes: Matches common desktop source-code inspection expectations without broad binary sniffing.

expected: A failed split job retains its intended first-part path so the containing folder remains discoverable.
notes: The user can resolve the collision without copying an opaque backend path from an error.

expected: The Job Center localizes the collision, explains the next action, and offers reveal-in-folder.
notes: Raw backend wording is not presented for the known recoverable conflict.

expected: A newly opened archive starts at its own root with an empty search and no selection inherited from the previous archive.
notes: The browser instance is keyed to the archive session handle.

expected: An invalid part size produces a localized field-level error linked to the input while Start remains disabled.
notes: The reason for the disabled action is visible and accessible.

expected: Empty input and path separators receive distinct localized field-level errors without losing the entered value.
notes: Users can correct the precise problem in place.

expected: Archive commands remain single-line and switch to compact icon presentation while preserving accessible names and tooltips.
notes: Prevents long localized labels from wrapping one character per line.

expected: The real desktop runtime keeps the browser and preview closed after a wrong password, explains the error, then recovers with the correct password.
notes: Covers the compiled WebView, Rust reader, integrity verification, password dialog, and retry path together.

expected: A correct password is not mislabeled as wrong when an encrypted stored entry cannot be authenticated; opening fails closed with a dedicated non-retryable error.
notes: Prevents an impossible password retry loop while refusing unauthenticated plaintext.

expected: The dedicated backend error is localized as an integrity limitation with an action that does not ask the user to retry the password.
notes: Keeps password and archive-format failures semantically distinct.

expected: The replacement session opens with the retained password before the old handle closes, and no redundant password dialog appears.
notes: A failed replacement open leaves the usable old session intact.

expected: A native source picker failure remains inside the append dialog as an actionable error without an unhandled rejection.
notes: The user can retry without reopening the workflow.

expected: A native output picker failure remains inside Archive tools as an actionable error without losing the selected operation.
notes: Covers save-dialog failure before a job is submitted.

expected: A native destination picker failure remains inside the extraction dialog as an actionable error.
notes: The extraction scope and conflict policy remain available.

expected: Rename cannot start without the password, forwards it to the typed job request, and clears it after success.
notes: Matches the delete flow and avoids retaining a secret in component state.

expected: Every selected sort direction and metadata preservation option is sent to the correct typed job command.
notes: Prevents command-name and camelCase payload drift.

expected: Each encrypted normalization command receives the password and the dialog clears it after every successful submission.
notes: The password is not retained between tool invocations.

expected: Append refuses to replace an archive changed by another writer and preserves the external writer's bytes.
notes: Exercises the fingerprint immediately before atomic replacement.

expected: Delete or rename transformation refuses to replace an archive changed by another writer and preserves the external writer's bytes.
notes: Covers the shared transform commit guard.

expected: Split reports completed source entries against the verified total instead of presenting every update as 100 percent.
notes: Uses a two-pass bounded-memory count before encoding.

expected: Part one is published last, so a failed split cannot leave a truncated prefix that concat discovers as complete.
notes: The surviving raced part is rejected because the part-one completeness marker is absent.

expected: Sort rejects a merge whose group-scoped metadata cannot be preserved instead of silently retaining only the first group's chunks.
notes: No separate output is published on the unsupported transformation.

expected: Header, chunk CRCs, and entry structure are checked while file contents are explicitly reported as not checked.
notes: Prevents quick verification from claiming complete payload coverage.

expected: Every file is read through its decode stream and receives item-level evidence with exact decoded byte totals.
notes: Verification writes no extracted files.

expected: A corrupt chunk produces an issues-found report and marks dependent checks as not checked instead of losing the result in an opaque job error.
notes: Uses a real serialized archive with a mutated payload byte.

expected: An encrypted stored entry passes only after its decoded plaintext matches the GUI integrity digest.
notes: No file-count password heuristic is used.

expected: A wrong password becomes failed item evidence after a real plaintext digest mismatch.
notes: Unauthenticated plaintext is never reported as checked.

expected: A decryption or decompression failure is retained as failed file evidence while the verification job completes with a report.
notes: Backend decoder wording is supplemental detail, not the result state.

expected: A correct password expands the solid group and reads every contained file.
notes: Covers the outer encrypted solid storage model.

expected: A solid decode failure is reported once and the non-advancing library iterator is not polled indefinitely.
notes: Prevents a hung verification worker.

expected: Encrypted stored content without deterministic integrity metadata is reported as not checked rather than passed or mislabeled as a wrong password.
notes: The factual conclusion is incomplete.

expected: An empty archive passes structural verification and states that no file content existed to read.
notes: No password inference is made from a zero file count.

expected: Directory entries are validated without inventing a file-content or password failure.
notes: Keeps non-file states factual.

expected: Complete verification reports a link as not restorable and the overall result as incomplete because the application always refuses link restoration.
notes: Matches the unconditional link-escape policy without offering an override.

expected: A failed item digest does not prevent a later independent file from being checked and reported.
notes: Both failed and passed evidence remain in one report.

expected: The serialized report retains at most 200 item checks, prioritizes failed and not-checked evidence, and states the omitted count.
notes: Prevents oversized IPC events and unbounded result DOM rendering.

expected: The recorded size and modification stamp distinguish the verified source from a later replacement or removal.
notes: The result UI can warn instead of presenting stale evidence as current.

expected: Cancellation returns Interrupted and is never converted into archive corruption evidence.
notes: The job manager maps the cancellation flag to its cancelled lifecycle.

expected: A completed background verification retains its structured report and exposes no output path.
notes: The report survives job-list reconciliation and can be reopened.

expected: A non-portable entry path becomes failed item evidence while the remaining entries are still verified.
notes: The report stays factual instead of aborting into an opaque job error.

expected: An unreadable container produces a failed archive-header check with the remaining checks marked not checked.
notes: Covers truncated downloads and renamed non-PNA files.

expected: The report states that entries after a failed solid group were not enumerated instead of claiming the archive has no file entries.
notes: Counts describe only what was actually enumerated.

expected: Complete verification of encrypted content without a password names the password as a possible cause in the failed evidence.
notes: A healthy archive is not silently misdiagnosed as damaged.

expected: A retried verification starts without a stale report and records a fresh one on success.
notes: Pins the snapshot reset in JobManager retry.

expected: When a modification time is unavailable the freshness query answers unknown instead of confirming a size-only match.
notes: Size alone cannot distinguish a same-size replacement.

expected: Check codes serialize as the snake_case strings the frontend union and label map expect.
notes: Pins the manually mirrored Rust-to-TypeScript contract.

expected: The dialog explains exactly what each mode checks before a typed background job is queued.
notes: No safety score or diagnostic marketing language is used.

expected: Quick verification needs no password; complete encrypted verification requires and forwards one without placing it in the report.
notes: The input has a concise accessible name and associated privacy hint.

expected: The result dialog shows factual counts and every item status without a risk score or safety claim.
notes: Uses a scrollable semantic list and localized core labels.

expected: A failed job start keeps the dialog open with an accessible error and allows retrying.
notes: Matches the error-recovery contract of the other job dialogs.

expected: When freshness cannot be confirmed the dialog says so instead of implying an unchanged source.
notes: The failure detail is shown with the notice for diagnosis.

expected: Verification starts from a labeled first-level archive command without navigating away from the browser.
notes: The default quick scope remains visible in the dialog.

expected: A completed verification remains in the job bar and never opens or replaces a result dialog without an explicit user action.
notes: Prevents background jobs from stealing focus or replacing evidence being read.

expected: A completed verification remains identifiable and its structured result can be reopened from the compact job drawer.
notes: No output-folder action is shown for a read-only operation.

expected: A succeeded verification job whose report found issues shows the failed-checks status instead of a generic success state.
notes: The job drawer signals corruption before the result dialog is opened.

expected: The typed verification job command is registered in the production Tauri handler and cannot expose an output path.
notes: Keeps frontend and Rust contracts deployable together.

expected: The compiled Tauri application reads real encrypted plaintext with the correct password and returns failed evidence for the wrong password.
notes: Covers command registration, job retention, libpna decryption, and serialized report shape.

expected: The evidence dialog remains bounded, scrollable, and actionable with long archive paths and a representative result list.
notes: Uses real Chromium layout rather than jsdom geometry.
